title: Berlin Airports - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin has one airport for scheduled passenger flights: BER.** Berlin's passenger air traffic has been concentrated at **Berlin Brandenburg Airport** since Tegel handled its final flight on **8 November 2020**.

**One airport, two terminals.** **Terminal 1** is the main building and currently includes easyJet; **Terminal 2** currently includes Ryanair and Wizz Air. They are about a five-minute signed walk apart. Check your booking because assignments can change. **Terminal 5** is permanently closed.

**Tegel and Schönefeld are former airports.** **Tegel (TXL)** closed on 8 November 2020. **Schönefeld (SXF)** became the now-closed T5; Schönefeld remains a place and an S-Bahn station, not a separate passenger airport.

**Getting into town is easy.** The **FEX Airport Express** runs about every 15 minutes through most of the day and reaches **Berlin Hauptbahnhof in around 23 minutes** via Südkreuz and Potsdamer Platz. The station is under Terminal 1 on level U2; follow the signed passage from T2.

**Buy an ABC ticket.** BER is in **fare zone C**, so any train to or from the airport needs a **Berlin ABC ticket** (**€5.00** single in 2026). Validate a paper ticket or activate a digital one before starting the journey.

**The old airports are worth knowing.** **Tempelhof Airport** closed in 2008 and its field opened as a public park in 2010. **Tegel** is being redeveloped, and the former Schönefeld terminal is closed. None of those old airport codes belongs on a current scheduled passenger booking.
